content.text:
## Implementation Plan

# Implementation Plan: Blog Feature

## Summary
Add a Blog model owned by an organization. Admins can manage blogs, and users can view them.

## PRD gaps compensated
None — PRD sections are aligned.

## Blueprint inventory
A blueprint YAML has been written for each of these models. Develop stage will run
`bundle exec rails agentcode:blueprint` to materialize them.

- `.agentcode/blueprints/_roles.yaml` — roles: admin, user
- `.agentcode/blueprints/blogs.yaml` — `Blog`

## Models (reference)

### `Blog`
- Ownership: organization-scoped
- Key fields: title, body, published
- Relationships: belongs_to Organization

## Extras (NOT covered by blueprint — develop stage implements these by hand)
None — blueprint covers every requirement.

## Screens
> **Rhino auto-generates index / show / create / update / destroy screens** for every model registered in `config.resources`.

## Key files reference
- `.agentcode/blueprints/blogs.yaml`
- `.agentcode/blueprints/_roles.yaml`

## Per-AC Implementation Map

| AC | Phase | Belongs to | Primary file(s) | Library / hook | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AC-1 | MVP | Blog Management | rhino-auto-generated `blogs` pages | useModelCreate(`blog`) | Blueprint scaffolds CRUD |
| AC-2 | MVP | Blog Viewing | rhino-auto-generated `blogs` pages | useModelIndex(`blog`) | Blueprint scaffolds CRUD |

## Implementation order (recommended stage flow)

1. **Plan** — this artifact set (YAMLs, plan, criteria, index).
2. **Develop** — phase A: `bundle exec rails agentcode:blueprint` + migrations. Phase B: implement **Extras**. Phase D: write backend unit + integration tests for every AC.
3. **UI / UI-Integration** — build screens listed above.
4. **Test** — run `bundle exec rails test` to confirm green and tighten anything broken.
5. **Review** — PRD compliance + test/coverage report.

---

## Original Task

## Previous Session Context

# Chat History — 2026-07-13T18-55-36

---

### [2026-07-13 19:23:04] Stage: plan

**Task:** Add a Blog model owned by an organization. Each blog has a title, body, and published boolean. Admins can create/edit/delete blogs. Users can view a list of blogs for their organization.

**Agent Response:**
I have completed the planning stage for the Blog feature.

The following artifacts have been created:
1. `.agentcode/blueprints/_roles.yaml` - Defines the `admin` and `user` roles.
2. `.agentcode/blueprints/blogs.yaml` - Defines the `Blog` model with `title`, `body`, and `published` fields, owned by an organization, with appropriate permissions for admins and users.
3. `.rhino-plan.md` - The implementation plan detailing the models, screens, and implementation order.
4. `.codalio/acceptance-criteria.json` - Contains the acceptance criteria for the feature.
5. `.codalio/index.json` - The project index file.

Wrote 2 criteria: 2 from PRD, 0 Extras, 0 gap-compensated.
Mapped 0 PRD functions to backend ops (0 flagged as gaps requiring scaffolded controls).

The develop stage can now proceed with running `bundle exec rails agentcode:blueprint` to generate the models and migrations.

### [2026-07-13 19:24:24] Stage: plan

**Task:** Add a Blog model owned by an organization. Each blog has a title, body, and published boolean. Admins can create/edit/delete blogs. Users can view a list of blogs for their organization.

**Agent Response:**
I have completed the planning stage for the Blog feature.

The following artifacts have been created:
1. `.agentcode/blueprints/_roles.yaml` - Defines the `admin` and `user` roles.
2. `.agentcode/blueprints/blogs.yaml` - Defines the `Blog` model with `title`, `body`, and `published` fields, owned by an organization. It also sets up the permissions for admins (full CRUD) and users (read-only).
3. `.rhino-plan.md` - The implementation plan detailing the models, screens, and implementation order.
4. `.codalio/acceptance-criteria.json` - Contains the acceptance criteria for the feature.
5. `.codalio/index.json` - The project index.

Wrote 2 criteria: 2 from PRD, 0 Extras, 0 gap-compensated.
Mapped 0 PRD functions to backend ops (0 flagged as gaps requiring scaffolded controls).

The develop stage can now proceed with running `bundle exec rails agentcode:blueprint` to generate the models and migrations.
